Three tier system- corporate (feel themselves like kings), region head quarter (feel themselves like ministers) and site (labour class)  . 

you find  people who join and retire in good places (corporate and head quarter), while many others languish in sites. For Finance and HR , the working is good - as they have no role - and good work life balance and posting in cities.

Hard working and deserving candidates are not encouraged - one person does the work of many, while the others rest and no action taken on them. Many times employees get transfers and the transfer are put on hold - as they dont want to go to site posting from city - dont know what the role of HR is - why transfers are made - purely lethargic and torture in terms on handling human resources.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Lots of responsibility given from early on but only if you are willing. 
Managerial Skills set start to grow at the entry level for executives due to a variety of jobs you need to get done by supervising a team.
Chance to interact and learn a lot from the service personnel of the best companies in the world who come to POWERGRID's sites for commissioning. 
Pay is quite good.
Being a Government Co., job is secure.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Work Life Balance is extremely poor fro those working in the sites.
